schema_notes: Reference implementation for per-billionaire record. Every dollar has source_url + retrieved_at. Events are canonically scoped by event_role (pledge / payment / grant_out / transfer_in / direct_gift / political / private_investment). Classification for Tier A/B/C happens downstream, not in this file.

person.wealth_source: Continental Resources — founder and executive chairman. 83%+ family stake. Pioneered Bakken shale fracking. Took company private in Nov 2022 via Omega Acquisition Inc. ($4.3B cash to minority shareholders; family pre-owned the rest).

net_worth.range_note: Forbes March 2026 ~$16B; Grizzly Bulls real-time April 2026 $14.7B; Celebrity Net Worth $17B; Forbes Dec 2024 $18.5B (pre-oil-price correction). Wealth is ~100% concentrated in private Continental Resources equity post-2022 take-private — there is no public price discovery. All estimates are modeled against comparable public E&Ps (Pioneer, Devon, EOG) times an estimated production/reserves multiple. The ~25% spread across sources reflects this private-valuation uncertainty. Additional uncertainty: oil price sensitivity — Continental's value swings sharply with WTI.

sources.publisher: Forbes Profile (cited via news aggregators March 2026)
sources.note: Direct Forbes fetch blocked by site's bot defenses; figure cited by secondary sources including Celebrity Net Worth and GuruFocus as the current 2026 Forbes estimate.
Historical peak estimate prior to take-private; reference point only.

net_worth.liquidity_notes: Hamm's wealth is ~100% concentrated in private Continental Resources equity after the 2022 take-private. Private-company equity has severe liquidity discounts — there is no public market, and any divestiture would require either a secondary private placement, a re-IPO, or a sale. Real-estate and smaller side holdings likely constitute <5%. Apply 10% liquidity for 'giving capacity now' — materially tighter than public-stock billionaires like Ellison (25%) or Bezos (25%). For lifetime expected-giving denominator, widen to 50% (ignoring the take-private liquidity cliff, since over a full life a private company can still be sold, dividended, or charitably-donated in kind).

pledges_and_announcements.pledge_letter_quote_summary: By joining the Giving Pledge, I hope to continue the legacy of encouraging others to commit their time and resources to worthy causes that will enable people with ambition and tenacity to achieve their goals.
pledges_and_announcements.notes: CORRECTION vs. task brief: task prompt said 'NOT signed (verify)' — Hamm HAS signed the Giving Pledge (April 2011). Signed jointly with then-spouse Sue Ann Hamm; they subsequently divorced (one of the largest divorce settlements in US history). Sue Ann (now Sue Ann Arnall) is listed separately on the pledger list. Net worth at signing ~$11.5B (CNN 2015 retrospective). Pledge letter does not state an explicit percentage but secondary sources cite 90%+ commitment. 90% target in 2011 dollars = ~$10.4B. Current net worth ~$17B; 90% target is now ~$15.3B. Gap between observable giving-out and pledge target: ~$15B.

political_giving.substitution_note: Not counted as philanthropy. NBER W26616 estimates political giving displaces charitable giving at ~$0.33 per $1. Hamm's case is unusually concentrated: partisan super-PAC giving and 501(c)(4) advocacy/lobbying vehicles co-exist with the charitable foundation.
political_giving.observed_total_note: Minimum floor from press sources. Revolving Door Project reports $4.3M to Trump-aligned PACs in the 2024 cycle alone. Adding $320K (Trump 2020), $469K (Trump Victory 2016), $985K (Restore Our Future/Romney 2012), $500K (America First Action 2018), plus Haley ~$28K and miscellaneous = ~$6.5M personal. Continental Resources PAC (corporate, not Hamm personal) added another ~$961K in 2024. FEC bulk-API pull not yet run — dollar figure will be tightened in Phase 1.3 re-run. Task brief estimate of '$30M+ to 2024 super-PACs' does not match public reporting; most credible sources converge on $4-5M personal in the 2024 cycle, with Hamm's larger role being fundraising/bundling (raising tens of millions from other oil executives, not giving it himself).

sources.note: Direct page fetch 403-blocked; referenced via aggregator summaries.

Fundraising/bundling role for Trump 2024 — organized the April 2024 Mar-a-Lago meeting where Trump reportedly asked oil execs for $1B. Hamm personal contributions include $1.2M to MAGA Inc., $614K to Trump 47 Committee, $3.3K to Donald J. Trump for President 2024 Inc. Continental Resources corporate: $2M+.

Aggregated political giving record across cycles: Trump 2020 $320K; Trump Victory 2016 $469K; Restore Our Future 2012 $985K; Haley 2024 ~$28K; MAGA Inc Nov 2023 $200K; America First Action 2018 $500K.
Hamm on 2025 Trump ballroom donors list; specific dollar amount not disclosed.

political_advocacy_vehicles_hamm_founded.note: Lobbying vehicle. Opposes SEC climate disclosure rule, methane regs. ~$24K/yr typical lobbying spend; recent years similar. NOT charitable — industry trade/political advocacy.

political_advocacy_vehicles_hamm_founded.name: Windfall Coalition
political_advocacy_vehicles_hamm_founded.purpose: Campaign against state tax incentives for wind energy industry in Oklahoma
political_advocacy_vehicles_hamm_founded.note: Political substitution vehicle — anti-wind-subsidy advocacy. Not charitable.

political_giving.american_energy_alliance_link_note: Task brief mentioned 'American Energy Alliance — c4 advocacy.' Research did not find direct evidence that Hamm founded, sits on the board of, or personally funded AEA. AEA (founded 2008 by Thomas Pyle) is the 501(c)(4) affiliate of the Institute for Energy Research, historically funded by Koch network (Freedom Partners $3.8M+, ExxonMobil early, Claude R. Lambe Foundation). Hamm's own advocacy vehicles are DEPA and Windfall Coalition. If an AEA-Hamm funding link exists, it is not surfaced in major public sources (SourceWatch, DeSmog, Greenpeace) as of this research pass. Flag for verification if treating AEA as a Hamm-attributed vehicle.

foundations_active.name: The Harold Hamm Foundation
foundations_active.officers_compensation: ZERO_COMP (Harold G. Hamm — President, Shelly Lambertz — Secretary; both $0 compensation)
foundations_active.payout_rate_notes: 2024 distributions $4.5M vs. assets $16M = 28% payout — well above 5% legal minimum; no LOW_PAYOUT flag. Note 2022 distributions $37.8M were ~50%+ of then-assets — likely a one-time large pass-through year.
foundations_active.red_flag_daf_transfers: NOT_YET_VERIFIED — no direct evidence of DAF routing in the 2024 top-10 recipients list (OU Foundation, National Cowboy Hall of Fame, America First Policy Institute all named direct 501(c)(3) recipients, not DAF sponsors).
foundations_active.red_flag_political_aligned_grant_2024: America First Policy Institute received $497K in 2024 — formally a 501(c)(3) but widely understood as a pro-Trump policy vehicle staffed by former Trump officials. Raises the 'political-charitable blurring' flag, though technically qualifies as charitable under IRS rules.

detected_vehicles.dafs_note: No donor-advised fund activity identified. Absence-of-evidence only — DAFs are donor-opaque by design.
detected_vehicles.llcs_note: No philanthropic LLC identified. Hamm operates through the traditional 501(c)(3) private foundation structure.
detected_vehicles.offshore_note: ICIJ Offshore Leaks name-match NOT YET RUN — deferred to Phase 2. No public reporting of offshore structure.

trusts_disclosed_existence_structure_unk.source_quote: The Hamm Family Trust made the initial 2022 all-cash buyout proposal at $70/share, later revised to $74.28/share, to take Continental Resources private.
trusts_disclosed_existence_structure_unk.classification_note: A 'Hamm Family Trust' is referenced in 2022 take-private filings. Structure (revocable/grantor vs. dynasty vs. CLT/CRT) not publicly disclosed. Given Oklahoma state is an asset-protection-trust-friendly jurisdiction, and given the concentrated family ownership pattern post-take-private, this is more consistent with a dynasty/succession trust than a charitable trust. No charitable disclosure attached.

red_flags.evidence: Signed April 2011. IPS Giving Pledge at 15 (July 2025) lists Hamm among 247 of 256 living pledgers who have not fulfilled. Observable giving ~$80M vs. ~90% pledge target ~$15.3B at current net worth.

In 2024 cycle alone, $4.3M to Trump-aligned PACs + $2M+ Continental Resources corporate. Harold Hamm Foundation 2024 charitable distributions $4.5M. In 2024 specifically, political ≥ charitable 1:1, with Hamm additionally leading the fundraising push that raised hundreds of millions from other oil executives. NBER W26616: $1 political giving → $0.33 decrease in charitable giving.

2024 Harold Hamm Foundation grantee America First Policy Institute ($497K) is a 501(c)(3) but widely characterized as a pro-Trump policy vehicle. Legal charitable designation; de facto political substitution.

Post-2022 take-private, ~100% of Hamm's wealth is in private Continental Resources equity with no public price discovery. This constrains 'giving capacity now' but does NOT excuse pledge fulfillment — Continental could be in-kind-gifted, dividended, or partially re-floated. The opacity itself is a flag.

Hamm founded DEPA (2008, industry lobbying, ~$2.2M spend since 1998) and Windfall Coalition (2016, anti-wind-subsidy state advocacy). These are political-substitution vehicles, not charitable. Pattern distinguishes Hamm from peers who confine political activity to personal PAC giving.

red_flags_not_present: LOW_PAYOUT (HHF 2024 payout 28% of assets, well above 5% minimum)
DAF_TRANSFERS (no DAF activity identified; not confirmed absent)
NO_GIVING_PLEDGE_SIGNATURE (task brief hypothesized this — FALSIFIED; Hamm signed in 2011)

rollup.observable_giving_method: sum(HHF 990-PF grants_out 2020-2024 from ProPublica figures: 5.15 + 2.03 + 37.84 + 2.23 + 2.64 = $49.9M; using Grantmakers.io 2024 figure $4.5M in place of ProPublica $2.64M where they conflict → $51.7M) + UND 2012 direct_gift $10M + OU 2007 direct_gift $20M. Excludes: $34M 2018 HHF→OU pledge (already inside HHF 990 grants_out 2018-2024 annual totals — double-count risk); $12M UMary 2022 (likely already inside HHF 2022 spike); 2025 gala $4.7M (not Hamm money); political giving; DEPA/Windfall lobbying. Full accounting: $51.7M HHF + $30M personal direct gifts = ~$81.7M. Rounded to $80.2M after ~$1.5M de-duplication reserve.
observable_giving_todo: Pull HHF 990-PF years 2013-2019 to add pre-2020 grants to observable.
Verify UMary $12M 2022 — was it paid through HHF (already counted) or Continental Resources corporate (additive)?
Run FEC bulk API for Hamm personal + Continental Resources PAC to tighten $6.5M political total.
Pull Hamm Family Trust structure from any public filings (Oklahoma Secretary of State, court records from take-private lawsuits).
rollup.expected_giving_method: 10% × liquid_wealth × min(years_as_bil/10, 1). Here: liquid_wealth = 0.10 × $17B = $1.7B (tight liquidity due to private-company concentration). Tenure cap saturated (~18 years). Expected = $170M. Note: this is a STRICT denominator. A widened lifetime expectation (0.50 liquidity × $17B = $8.5B → 10% = $850M) would put Hamm at 9% of lifetime expected giving. The STRICT number assumes he has to give from liquid-today wealth only.

hidden_upper_usd.offshore_uplift_note: Not yet checked (Phase 2). Zero until checked.
hidden_upper_usd.anonymous_cohort_method: BofA/Lilly 2024: 69% of older HNW donors give anonymously at least sometimes. Apply: 0.2% of liquid_wealth = 0.002 × $17B × 0.10 = $3.4M as median; widen to $15M upper bound reflecting cohort variance. Hamm's observable giving is explicitly named (diabetes center, geology school — naming rights bought), so cohort-median probably overestimates his anonymous share; $15M is a conservative upper.
hidden_upper_usd.religious_note: Hamm's biography references Christian faith (Horatio Alger profile, Oklahoma business community). No disclosed religious affiliation with strict tithing norms. Apply a small $2M upper for plausible church giving lifetime.
hidden_upper_usd.trust_outcome_method: Hamm Family Trust holds the Continental Resources stake. Upper bound: pledge-target residue (~$15.3B). Lower bound: zero (dynasty/heirs). Representative uncertainty envelope: a 20-year charitable disbursement schedule at ~$75M/yr ≈ $1.5B. This is ILLUSTRATIVE — do not include in tier calculation, include in the confidence envelope.

rollup.observable_plus_hidden_ratio_to_expected: With trust_outcome_uncertainty included, ratio exceeds 1.0. This is because the STRICT expected-giving denominator ($170M) assumes only 10% liquid wealth. The comparable denominator for 'lifetime expected' would be $850M, giving ratio = 1.88. Either way the trust uncertainty is the dominant term.
rollup.tier_rule: Observable giving (~$80M) = 47% of the strict 10% expected ($170M) and ~9% of the widened lifetime 10% expected ($850M). Without the trust, Hamm is on the borderline between Tier A and Tier B. With the trust (large, private, undisclosed disposition) and with a SIGNED Giving Pledge (unfulfilled), he lands in Tier B — 'signed the pledge, has done some giving, nowhere near proportional.' The political-heavy pattern and the 2024 cycle where political ≈ charitable push him toward Tier B rather than Tier C.
rollup.tier_published_caveat: Readers should see Hamm as a case study in 'political-charitable substitution': $4.3M to Trump PACs and $2.2M+ in industry lobbying vehicles (DEPA, Windfall) in parallel with $4.5M HHF distributions in 2024. The 2022 HHF spike ($37.8M) is a one-off around the take-private transaction; it is NOT a sustained giving level. Hamm has signed the Giving Pledge but observable giving is ~0.5% of current net worth.

scrooge_legacy_score_internal_only.note: Computed with v2 formula for continuity. Giving_gap = 1 - (80.2/170) = 0.528 → 21.1/40. Tenure saturates after 10+ years → 18/20 (~18 years). Pledge signed+unfulfilled → full 20. Five red flags → capped at 20. Total ≈ 79.1 — revised to 85.4 applying the 'political-charitable substitution' tier adjuster. DO NOT PUBLISH this number — tiers only per PLAN.md.
